fields.name: Carbonara
fields.steps: Cook the pasta according to package instructions until al dente. Reserve 1/2 cup of the cooking water before draining the pasta. While the pasta is cooking, cook the pancetta in a large skillet over medium heat until crisp, about 8 minutes. Remove the pancetta with a slotted spoon and set aside. In a medium bowl, whisk together the egg yolks, Parmesan cheese, and black pepper. Add the pasta to the skillet with the pancetta and toss to combine. Remove the skillet from the heat and let cool for 1-2 minutes. Gradually pour the egg mixture into the pasta, stirring constantly, until the pasta is coated in a creamy sauce. If the sauce is too thick, add a little bit of the reserved pasta water to thin it out. Serve immediately, garnished with additional Parmesan cheese and black pepper, if desired.

fields.name: Margarita
fields.steps: Preheat your oven to 475°F (245°C). Make the pizza dough: combine flour, salt, and yeast in a large bowl. Stir in the water and mix until a sticky dough forms. Knead the dough for a few minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic. Cover with a damp towel and let it rest for 10 minutes. Divide the dough in half and shape each piece into a ball. Flatten the dough balls and stretch them out into 12-inch circles. Place the dough circles on a lightly floured baking sheet or pizza stone. Spread a layer of tomato sauce on each pizza crust. Add a layer of mozzarella cheese on top of the tomato sauce. Sprinkle some fresh basil leaves on top of the cheese. Bake the pizzas in the preheated oven for 12-15 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the cheese is melted and bubbly. Remove the pizzas from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes. Slice and serve the pizzas hot.

fields.name: Caesar
fields.steps: In a large bowl, whisk together 2 egg yolks, 2 minced garlic cloves, 1 teaspoon Dijon mustard, 2 tablespoons lemon juice, and 1/4 teaspoon kosher salt until smooth. While whisking constantly, slowly drizzle in 1/2 cup extra-virgin olive oil until the mixture is thick and creamy. Add 1/2 cup finely grated Parmesan cheese, 8 cups chopped romaine lettuce, and 1 cup croutons to the bowl with the dressing. Toss until everything is evenly coated in the dressing. Season with black pepper and additional kosher salt to taste. Serve immediately.

fields.name: Hamburger Burger
fields.steps: Preheat grill to high heat. In a large bowl, mix together the ground beef, onion, cheese, soy sauce, Worcestershire sauce, egg, onion soup mix, garlic, garlic powder, parsley, basil, oregano, black pepper, and salt. Form the mixture into 6 patties. Grill the patties for 5 minutes per side, or until well done. Serve on buns with your favorite toppings, such as lettuce, tomato, onion, ketchup, mustard, and mayonnaise.

fields.name: Chicken Soup
fields.steps: Start by heating a large pot over medium heat. Add oil, onions, carrots, and celery. Cook for a few minutes until the vegetables start to soften.  Add garlic and ginger and cook for another minute.  Pour in chicken broth and add chicken breasts. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let simmer until chicken is cooked through, about 15-20 minutes.  Remove chicken from the pot and shred it using two forks. Set aside.  Add noodles to the pot and cook according to package instructions.  Once the noodles are cooked, add the shredded chicken back to the pot along with any other desired seasonings, such as salt, pepper, and thyme.  Stir in chopped parsley and serve hot.

fields.name: Fried Chicken
fields.steps: Place the chicken pieces in a large bowl and add the buttermilk, salt, and hot sauce. Mix well to coat the chicken pieces evenly. In a separate bowl, mix together the flour, salt, black pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, and paprika. Remove each chicken piece from the buttermilk mixture, allowing any excess liquid to drip off, and coat it in the flour mixture. Shake off any excess flour and place the chicken on a wire rack. Heat a large, heavy-bottomed pot over medium-high heat and add enough oil to come up about 2 inches in the pot. Heat the oil until it reaches 350°F. Add the chicken pieces to the hot oil, being careful not to overcrowd the pot. Fry the chicken until it is golden brown and crispy on the outside and the internal temperature reaches 165°F, about 10-12 minutes. Use a slotted spoon to remove the chicken from the oil and transfer it to a wire rack to drain any excess oil. Serve the fried chicken hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
